ops.title: train extra uninstallable on 3.14t (tinker→orjson has no free-threaded wheel)
ops.body:
## Finding (2026-07-14, during v0.4.0 ship)

`uv pip install experiment-at-home[train]` **fails on free-threaded CPython 3.14t**. The `train` extra pulls `tinker>=0.22` → `orjson` (3.11.9), which has no `cp314t` wheel and fails to build from source (maturin/cargo exit 101 under PYO3 for the free-threaded ABI). The failure is wholesale: because the extra is monolithic (`tinker`, `modal`, `datasets` in one), even a user who only wants the modal or local path can't install `[train]` on 3.14t.

## Why it doesn't block 0.4.0
- `train` is opt-in; CI installs it only on 3.13 / 3.14 (GIL) jobs, where it resolves cleanly. The 3.14t core-import job syncs `dev` only.
- `athome.train.tinker` imports `tinker` lazily (`TYPE_CHECKING` + `importlib.util.find_spec` in `available()`), so `import athome.train` still works on 3.14t; `TinkerBackend.available()` just returns False and `select()` falls through.
- The **local** backend is 3.14t-native regardless: it drives `uvx --from mlx-lm` as an out-of-process sidecar, so it needs nothing from the `train` extra's in-venv deps.
- The paid smokes therefore run on a dedicated **3.13** venv (`experiment-at-home[train]` installs fine there).

## Practical statement for consumers
On the stream-trade 3.14t runtime, `athome.train` is effectively **local-only** (mlx-lm sidecar). Any consumer that must run **tinker or modal** training needs a GIL-enabled interpreter (3.13 or 3.14). This matters for Phase E (cc-steer retrain uses tinker) and Phase F (write-like-me trainer) — check each consumer's interpreter before wiring the tinker/modal path.

## Open follow-up (candidate athome point release during E/F)
Split the monolithic `train` extra so `modal` and `local` install independently of `tinker` on 3.14t — e.g. `train-tinker` (tinker) vs `train-modal` (modal, datasets). UNVERIFIED whether `modal`/`datasets` alone build on 3.14t; confirm before committing to the split. Ties to the plan's "athome point releases as E/F surface gaps."
